sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Torino, currently sitting at 11th in the league with 44 points, welcomes Inter, who are in a strong 2nd position with 74 points, to the Stadio Olimpico Grande Torino in Turin. This matchup promises to be a challenging one for the home side, as they face a formidable Inter team under the watchful eye of referee Federico La Penna, known for averaging 5.1 yellow cards per game. The stage is set for an intriguing battle between a mid-table side and a title contender.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Inter have dominated with 5 wins, leaving Torino without a single victory or draw. Their most recent encounter on October 5, 2024, saw Inter edge out Torino 3-2 at home, with Marcus Thuram earning the Player of the Match title with an outstanding 9.5 Sofascore rating. Inter have been the first to score in all of the last 5 head-to-head games, and they hold an unbeaten streak of 11 matches against Torino.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Inter, Marcus Thuram stands out as a major threat, having scored 14 goals this season, ranking him 3rd in the league, alongside 4 assists and a high average Sofascore rating of 7.31. Hakan Çalhanoğlu, with the highest average rating in the league at 7.42, is another pivotal player for Inter, contributing 5 goals and 4 assists. Federico Dimarco also shines with 16 big chances created, topping the league in this metric. On Torino’s side, Che Adams leads the attack with 9 goals and 3 assists, showing a positive xG overperformance of 0.9231. Nikola Vlašić, with 5 goals and 4 assists, will be crucial in midfield for the home team.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Inter come into this game with a streak of 3 matches without a loss, showcasing their strong form. Torino, on the other hand, have struggled to score, with their last 7 home games producing less than 2.5 goals. Torino have also been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 home matches, which could spell trouble against a potent Inter side. Both teams have seen less than 4.5 cards in their last 5 respective games, suggesting a potentially disciplined encounter.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: Torino have scored 39 goals this season, ranking 12th in the league, but they’ve underperformed their expected goals by -6.33, placing them 16th in xG over/underperformance. Despite this, they’ve been solid defensively at home, conceding the 7th least goals in the league with 40. Inter, meanwhile, are lethal in the final stages of matches, with 33.33% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Torino tend to peak earlier, scoring 25% of their goals between the 31st and 45th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Squad Depth
sections.text: Torino will miss key midfielder Samuele Ricci, who has a goal and an assist this season with a 6.88 average rating, alongside defender Borna Sosa. Inter, however, will be without defender Benjamin Pavard, who has a 7.02 average rating. Despite these absences, Inter’s squad depth remains impressive, with in-form players like Denzel Dumfries, who has 2 goals and 3 assists in his last 5 matches with a 7.65 average Sofascore rating.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: With referee Federico La Penna officiating, who averages 5.1 yellow cards and 0.3 red cards per game, we can expect a few cautions in this match. Torino have accumulated 76 yellow cards this season, ranking 15th for the least in the league, while their games average 2.17 yellow cards. Both teams have seen less than 10.5 corners in recent matches, with Torino averaging 4.09 corners per game, ranking 16th in the league.
